title: Accessible Content Models, Landmarks, Headings, Links, Media, and Language · Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for accessible content models, landmarks, headings, links, media, and language and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Enforce document language, unique main, named navigation, logical heading outline, bypass mechanism, title, and meaningful sequence.
Require meaningful link text, current-location evidence, multiple ways to find pages, breadcrumb semantics, and non-color state.
Validate image purpose, alt decision, captions, transcripts, audio description needs, decorative handling, and accessible fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Landmark Heading Model · changed case · Quiz Landmark Heading Mod…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz publishing team handles case 6825 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then publish below a project subpath and verify every internal URL, fragment, asset, feed, and canonical. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

At Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Accessibility Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A quiz publishing team handles case 3489 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.

For Static-site publishing and Python Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Accessibility Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: An automated accessibility scanner can certify the whole static site.

stimulus.title: Accessibility Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: An automated accessibility scanner can certify the whole static site.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.

options.text: Run the Accessibility Evidence boundary case for WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.

Accept the Accessibility Evidence result because visible WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate output is the highest authority. That claim fails the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary for Accessibility Evidence.

For diagnose the hidden failure, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof. This does not satisfy Accessibility Evidence for WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Accessibility Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Accessibility Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 1870 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate generated page structure and content on WCAG evidence and human reader tasks; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Accessibility Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieving Accessibility Evidence through the concrete WCAG 2.2 content and template conformance gate case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Accessibility Evidence, keep Accessibility Evidence connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine structural checks, keyboard and zoom review, screen-reader tasks, content review, changed routes, and human ownership.
